name: Pico del Alto Rey — circular via the old path of Alto Rey from Bustares
name_original: Pico del Alto Rey — circular por el camino viejo del Alto Rey desde Bustares
region_intro: Hiking around Bustares offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, characterized by mountain ranges, river gorges, and historic villages. The region features prominent peaks like Alto Rey Peak, which reaches 1,858 meters, providing significant elevation changes for hikers. Natural features include the Barranco del Río Bornova and the Río Pera Galena, contributing to varied terrain. This area provides a tranquil setting for outdoor activities, with trails winding through both natural and historically significant areas. Best hiking trails around Bustares The most popular hiking route is Pico del Alto Rey — circular via the old path of Alto Rey from Bustares, a 10.2 miles (16.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends to Alto Rey Peak, offering expansive views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Viewpoint to the Bear Cave from Prádena de Atienza, a moderate 6.0 miles (9.6 km) path. This trail leads to a viewpoint overlooking the Cueva del Oso, traversing varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Hiendelaencina – Hiendelaencina Silver Mines loop from Hiendelaencina, a 5.9 miles (9.4 km) trail leading through the historic mining area, often completed in about 2 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Bustares is defined by mountain ascents, river valleys, and trails through historic mining landscapes. The network offers options for easy walks, moderate excursions, and challenging mountain routes. The routes in Bustares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 9 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Bustares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Bustares?
A: Bustares, located in the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, offers a diverse range of hiking trails. You'll find everything from challenging mountain ascents to the prominent Alto Rey Peak, to moderate excursions through river valleys like the Barranco del Río Bornova, and easier walks through historic mining landscapes. The area features both circular and linear routes.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Bustares?
A: Yes, while many routes offer significant elevation changes, there are options suitable for families and those seeking easier walks. The region's diverse terrain includes trails that are less demanding, allowing for a relaxed exploration of the natural beauty and historic areas. Currently, there is 1 easy route available, and 6 moderate routes that might be suitable for families depending on their experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Bustares?
A: The Sierra Norte de Guadalajara region is generally welcoming to dogs, making it a great place for hiking with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to check specific trail regulations or local signage for any restrictions.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or viewpoints to see while hiking?
A: The region boasts several impressive natural features. You can hike to the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4187789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey Peak (1,858 m)&lt;/a&gt; for expansive views, or explore the area around the Barranco del Río Bornova and La Junta de los Ríos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Pelagallinas River&lt;/a&gt; are also a highlight, known for its refreshingly cool waters in summer. Another interesting point is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cueva del Oso&lt;/a&gt;, which can be reached via trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030368968/viewpoint-to-the-bear-cave-from-pradena-de-atienza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint to the Bear Cave from Prádena de Atienza&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions accessible from the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can explore the 19th-century mining heritage in &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4681084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt;, which features a Silver Museum and monuments dedicated to the mining industry. The village of Bustares itself is a beautiful, quiet town with medieval origins, an 11th-century church, and an ancient hermitage. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14886053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina – Hiendelaencina Silver Mines loop from Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt; for a direct connection to this history.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bustares?
A: Bustares offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, ideal for exploring. Summer can be warm, but the Río Pera Galena offers cool waters for refreshment. Winter hikes are possible, especially at lower elevations, but higher peaks like Alto Rey may require appropriate gear due to snow and colder conditions.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in the Bustares area. For example, you can undertake the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030383946/pico-del-alto-rey-circular-via-the-old-path-of-alto-rey-from-bustares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Alto Rey — circular via the old path of Alto Rey from Bustares&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a rewarding ascent to the peak. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14886053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina – Hiendelaencina Silver Mines loop from Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt;, which combines natural beauty with historical exploration.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bustares?
A: The hiking routes in Bustares are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 stars from more than 9 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil setting, the diverse landscapes ranging from mountain peaks to river gorges, and the opportunity to combine natural exploration with visits to historic villages and mining sites.

Q: Is parking available at trailheads in Bustares?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Bustares and at common starting points for trails. For popular routes, such as those leading to Alto Rey Peak or the Cueva del Oso, designated parking areas are usually present in nearby villages like Bustares or Prádena de Atienza. It's advisable to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.

Q: How can I get to Bustares using public transport?
A: Public transport options to Bustares and the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara region can be limited. Bus services connect some of the larger towns in the province, but direct routes to smaller villages like Bustares may be infrequent. It's recommended to check local bus schedules in advance, as a car often provides the most flexible access to the various trailheads in the area.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for day hiking on established trails within the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, fire restrictions, and private land. If you plan to engage in activities like camping or large group events, it's best to consult the local authorities or park management for any specific requirements.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay overnight in Bustares?
A: Bustares, being a quiet and historic village, offers some amenities for visitors. You can find local cafes or restaurants to refuel after a hike. For accommodation, there are rural houses and guesthouses available, providing a peaceful retreat. Nearby villages may also offer additional options for dining and lodging, contributing to the tranquil experience of the region.
tips:
Mountain of the Central System located in the homonymous mountain range, to the north-northwest of the province of Guadalajara. Its summit is a succession of small slopes aligned along 3.5 km.
At its top is the hermitage of Santo Alto Rey, the object of a religious procession on the first Saturday of September from the towns that are on its slopes, as well as a military station at its western end and four Retevisión antennas next to the hermitage.
Religious sanctuary located in the Gascueña municipality at the foot of the Alto Rey peak in the homonymous mountain range.

waypoints.name: Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción
